# A Tiny Toy Transcriber

## Lesson Goal

Split clean synthetic audio into fixed segments and detect pitches.

## What to Notice

This is a complete miniature transcription pipeline with intentionally unrealistic assumptions.

## Work With the Notebook

- [Open the notebook source](https://github.com/buildLittleWorlds/marimo-audio-self-tutorial/blob/main/notebooks/12_simple_transcriber/notebook.py)
- [Run the browser notebook](https://buildlittleworlds.github.io/marimo-audio-self-tutorial/notebooks/12_simple_transcriber/)

Run locally:

```bash
uv run marimo edit notebooks/12_simple_transcriber/notebook.py
```

## Feedback Lens

A good student explanation separates the toy assumptions from real-world AMT challenges.

## Instructor Notes

Use this lesson as a small checkpoint. Before asking students to add features,
ask them to name the input, the transformation, the output, and the current
limitation. That habit will matter more in later transcription notebooks than
any one line of code.
